widgetData.source_sentence: Where do I find the library structure information in the WM SYSTEM ADMINISTRATOR’S GUIDE
sentences:
WMi SYSTEM ADMINISTRATOR’S GUIDE
Copyright © 2023 Manhattan Associates, Inc. All rights reserved. 39
Chapter 7: Understanding WM Library
Structure
This chapter discusses the IBM i WM library structure, including a discussion of setting up user profiles.
Library Overview
On the IBM i system, a library is a place where related objects are organized and stored. Every object
must be located in a library. Objects of the same type that are located in the same library cannot have the
same name. You can move objects from library to library, but you must rename a moved object if an
object of the same type and name already exists in that library. Failure to give objects of the same type a
unique name can cause the existing object to be overwritten. Since a library is also an object, you can
create, copy, and delete them.
Viewing and Editing the Current Library List
When carrying out a job on the IBM i, the order in which the system searches the libraries is based on the
order of the library list. When looking for an object, the system starts at the top of the library list and looks
through all libraries until it finds the first occurrence of the object. The search starts with the lowest
sequence number. The command to view and change the current library list is EDTLIBL.
To view the current library list:
1. At the command line, type EDTLIBL and press <Enter>.
The following table outlines the information in the Edit Library List screen:
Table 17: Edit Library List Screen
Column Description
Sequence
Number
The corresponding library’s' order in the
current library list. For example, the system
library QTEMP, which has a sequence number
of 020, comes before WM$$$BASP, which has
a sequence number of 030.
Library The library name.

APPENDIX B - MINISOFT
Copyright © 2023 Manhattan Associates, Inc. All rights reserved.
4
5. Read the license terms, select the I accept the terms of the license agreement check box, and
click Next.
6. Specify a destination folder to install eFORMz to on your location machine. The default location is
C:\Minisoft. Click Next.

Term: Ticketing
Definition: The process of applying customer-specific labels, price marking, and other indications to merchandise being shipped. This may require a special area and special considerations in material flow. May include removing vendor labels.

Term: Incoterms
Definition: Maintained by the International Chamber of Commerce (ICC), this codification of terms is used in foreign trade contracts to define which parties incur the costs and at what specific point the costs are incurred.

Term: Store Pack
Definition: Store pack locations are locations used to perform the process known as ‘Put to Store’. The only WM customers who use store pack locations are those who bridge store distributions to WM instead of picktickets. These are typically (but not exclusively) retailers or operators of ‘flow through’ facilities. Store pack locations are dedicated either to a store or a department within a store.

Comparison Operators
Comparison Operators
Operator Description
EQ (=) Equal
GE (>=) Greater Than or Equal To
GT (>) Greater Than
IN ({n,m}) Value In List
LE (<=) Less Than or Equal To
LT (<) Less Than
NE (<>) Not Equal
NI (No {n,m}) Value Not In List
59	Work With Options

Topic: picking packing
  → Picking and Packing (/Content/ProcessFlows/Outbound/PickPack.htm#kanchor242)
  Sub-topic: ERD
    → Pick Pack Process ERD (/Content/ERDs/ERD_PickPackProcess.htm#kanchor811)

Term: Picking
Definition: The process by which merchandise is selected in piece, carton or pallet quantities to satisfy demand requirement for a shipping order. Picking is generally done from active storage locations, but may be done from reserve locations (pick-from-reserve).

This is where you pick which parameters will be visible to or changeable by the user when the job is ran.
Since this job will be run in the background by FormWeaver, we will want to set each one to 3 for Non-display.
The quickest way to do this is to press F15 twice. Press ENTER to create your new job. You will see the new
job in the list.

Term: Warehouse Master
Definition: WM tables that define and control the basic operations of WM. The Warehouse Master allows you to customize WM to match your specific business and operational preferences. All of the following tables contain information defined using the Warehouse Master option: • Warehouse Master Table • Warehouse General Parameters Table • Warehouse Inbound Parameters Table • Warehouse Outbound Parameters Table

Term: Export License
Definition: A government document (also known as an "Individual Validated License") authorizing exports of specific goods in specific quantities to a particular destination. This document may be required in some countries for most or all exports and in other countries only under special circumstances.

Term: Allocable
Definition: An inventory state in WM that refers to Business System (host) inventory that is available for allocation or assignment to customer orders. Also referred to as Available to Sell.

Term: Business Host
Definition: An enterprise computer responsible for controlling business functions such as purchasing, accounts payable, general ledger, and perpetual inventory information.

Term: Wave
Definition: A discrete set of activities with a clearly defined start and stop point. May refer to picking, packing, shipping, or replenishment. Generally, a wave has to do with the orderly use of a limited resource, that is, a shipping wave may be defined as the set of orders which fill the shipping dock space available for cases. A picking wave could be the picking necessary for a shipping wave.
